name: Economic Community of West African States
climate: Tropical to semi-arid; Sahel zone in the north, humid tropical along the coast, savanna in the interior

neighbors.name: Sahel
neighbors.relevance: Northern ECOWAS members (Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso) overlap with the Sahel; security and climate risks spill over

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: Parent continental body; ECOWAS is a recognized AU regional economic community

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Major trade partner and development donor; Economic Partnership Agreements govern trade terms

neighbors.name: Mediterranean Sea
neighbors.relevance: Proximity drives migration routes and trade links to North Africa and Europe

neighbors.name: Belt and Road Initiative
neighbors.relevance: Chinese infrastructure investment in ports (Lekki, Kribi) and railways across West Africa

links.description: All ECOWAS members are AU members; AfCFTA integration builds on ECOWAS trade liberalization
Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso span both blocs; Sahel security crisis drives ECOWAS intervention
Nigeria is a major OPEC producer; oil revenues dominate regional economics
EU is the largest export destination; cocoa, oil, and minerals flow north under EPA frameworks
Intra-African trade corridor; West-to-East trade growing under AfCFTA
Coastal shipping lane for oil and container exports; piracy hotspot requiring naval cooperation
